item.title: In pictures: The US celebrates its 250th birthday
item.description: The anniversary, known as Independence Day, featured huge parades, colourful fireworks and plenty of events in towns and cities throughout America.

item.title: How did YOU watch the England game?
item.description: Whether you woke up early to catch-up, had a special watch party at your school or just caught the highlights - we want to know how YOU watched the epic match? Take part in our vote and let us know in

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are married!
item.description: A spokesperson for the couple confirmed the news after the ceremony, which took place on Friday. Find out more here.

item.title: Argentina stage stunning late comeback to beat Egypt in World Cup last 16
item.description: ‌Holders Argentina survive huge scare to earn a 3-2 win ⁠over Egypt and reach the World Cup quarterfinals.

item.title: Earth’s crust under Africa is thinning in a way never seen before
item.description: Africa’s Turkana Rift Zone has been caught in the act of “necking" — a key point in breakup of continental crust.

item.title: VR can train the human brain to treat wings like real limbs
item.description: Virtual "flying" coaching, based on bird flight, took only a week. Afterward, participants’ brains seemed to view VR wings almost like true limbs.

item.title: The Hastings Museum Kool-Aid Exhibit in Hastings, Nebraska
item.description: Hastings, Nebraska is where Edwin Perkins invented Kool-Aid. To honor his delicious invention, the local museum has dedicated half of a floor to the drink’s history. Kool-Aid: Discover the Dream is a

item.title: Viral Kindness Campaign Leaves Surprises on Cars of Anyone Who Took Cabs Home from the Bar
item.description: It’s an early-summer’s night and you left your truck at the bar because you chose not to drive drunk—well someone’s noticed. That someone is the Montana Barfairies, a volunteer effor

item.title: Female Rower Completes Record-Breaking Solo Journey from California to Hawaii
item.description: As America celebrated its 250th Birthday, Kelsey Pflendler celebrated her own significant milestone—becoming the first American woman to row a boat from California to Hawaii alone. As she pulled into

item.title: Ugandan Coffee Growers Shrug Off Drought Thanks to Regenerative Agriculture
item.description: Among the rolling hills of Uganda’s Masaka region, robusta coffee plants are producing larger, tastier yields thanks to a pilot program utilizing regenerative agriculture to battle droughts or e

item.title: Rooftop Solar Gave Power to Pakistan When Fossil Fuels Failed– it’s Now Called a Boom
item.description: Battered by energy shocks, private Pakistani investment in rooftop solar panels has skyrocketed to the point that they now contribute one-fifth of the total national power supply. The Guardian reviewe

item.title: Shell’s New Concept EV Designed to Charge in Under 10 Minutes and Run at 6 miles per kWh
item.description: Shell have unveiled an all-electric concept car to inspire the next generation of European-made mass-market EVs by focusing on lighter weights and better charging technology. Known as the “Tripl

item.title: Blind People Offered Hope of Seeing Again After Stem Cell Breakthrough Remakes Retinal Blood Vessels
item.description: Blind people have been offered fresh hope of seeing again after lab-grown cells restored retinal function in mice. When injected into mice with retinal disease, the special retinal blood cells integra

item.title: 'I can lift cars... plural': The Rock answers YOUR questions
item.description: Ahead of the new live-action Moana movie's release, we sent three press packers to speak to Dwayne Johnson who plays Maui to ask him YOUR questions.

item.title: €12 Million Portrait of Picasso’s ‘Golden Muse’ Recovered from Thieves After Paris Theft
item.description: A police raid on suspected drug dealers turned up a stolen painting by Pablo Picasso worth tens of millions of dollars. That’s because it is reported to depict Picasso’s “golden muse

item.title: Surprise Audio Played at Wedding Shocks Everyone When Bride Accurately Predicts Husband’s Name as a 4-Year-old
item.description: The surprise was waiting at the wedding reception. Max Creamer and Jordan Rosenberg met at a Halloween party about ten years ago when they were in college at San Diego State University. They got engag

item.title: Neighbors Pull Grandma Out of Burning Home But Looked Like ‘Drunk Thugs’ in Doorbell Camera Alert Sent to Daughter
item.description: Hero neighbors raced into a burning home to save an 87-year-old grandma after her daughter watched the terrifying fire on a doorbell camera. Phyllis Day, who has Alzheimer’s, was asleep when a blaze b

item.title: What are the mysterious 'space balls' found washed up on an Australian beach?
item.description: The strange silver balls are thought to have come from space and people have been warned not to touch them. Find out more here.

item.title: Crow Takes to Teaching a Starving Orphan Bird How to Eat (Watch)
item.description: A starving baby crow with barely any feathers is being nursed back to health—and another older bird has taken him under his wing. The tiny English crow known as the jackdaw was found in a “sorry state

item.title: Rare 1776 Declaration of Independence Discovered by National Archives in UK–One of Only 11 Known to Survive
item.description: A rare 1776 printing of the US Declaration of Independence — one of just 11 known to survive and the only copy outside the United States — has been discovered by a volunteer sorting historical letters
